rationale: Knowledge retrieval (RAG) adds latency and tokens. This config enforces strict activation conditions and similarity floors to ensure only high-signal content enters the context.

ranking.rationale: Top-5 is sufficient for most technical queries. Reranking is skipped to avoid additional latency on Free Tier.

freshness.rationale: Chunks older than 6 months may reference deprecated APIs or outdated architecture. Age penalty applied in scoring.

chunk_strategy.rationale: 512-token chunks balance retrieval precision with context budget. 64-token overlap prevents boundary truncation of critical sentences.

retrieval_budget.rationale: 1500 tokens from RAG provides substantial domain grounding without crowding the answer space. 25% context cap prevents RAG from displacing reasoning capacity.

citation.rationale: Citations allow the user to verify retrieved content and detect outdated chunks. Mandatory — never present retrieved content as original reasoning.

fallback.rationale: Model parametric knowledge is often accurate but may be stale. The verify tag signals the user to check official documentation for time-sensitive facts.

examples: API breaking change after training cutoff
web_search_override.rationale: Web search overrides RAG for time-sensitive facts where static collections are guaranteed stale.
